description:
You are a tier-1 IT support technician who has just received an internal request to finish processing yesterday’s work queue.  
    A small SQLite database already exists on the system:

    • Path: /home/user/helpdesk/helpdesk.db  
    • Table: tickets  
      – id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
      – title TEXT  
      – status TEXT (allowed values: open, in_progress, resolved, closed)

    Current dataset:
        id | title                     | status
        -----------------------------------------
         1 | Cannot connect to VPN     | open
         2 | Email not syncing         | open
         3 | Forgot password           | resolved
         4 | Printer jam               | in_progress

    Your assignment:
    1. Change the status of ticket id 4 from in_progress to resolved.  
    2. Produce a brief text report that shows the current counts of tickets in each status category after the update.

    Required report:
    • Path: /home/user/helpdesk/ticket_summary.log  
    • File format (exactly 4 lines, no extra spaces, no extra blank lines):
        Total Tickets: <integer>
        Open Tickets: <integer>
        In-Progress Tickets: <integer>
        Resolved Tickets: <integer>

      Use singular/plural capitalization exactly as shown. Values must be derived directly from the database after your update.

    Example of the expected layout (values will differ):
        Total Tickets: 12
        Open Tickets: 3
        In-Progress Tickets: 2
        Resolved Tickets: 7

    Constraints:
    • Solve the task using standard SQLite command-line tools already available in the container.  
    • Only two or three terminal commands should be enough (one UPDATE, one or two SELECT/redirect).  
    • Do not hard-code numbers—query the database.  
    • The agent does not have root privileges; keep all work inside /home/user.  

    The automated grader will fail the task if:
    • The database is not updated correctly (ticket id 4 must show status “resolved”).  
    • /home/user/helpdesk/ticket_summary.log is missing, extra-formatted, or contains incorrect counts.  
    • The file contains any extra lines, characters, or spacing different from the required format.
truth:
Initial system state before the agent starts:

    1. Directory /home/user/helpdesk exists (permissions 755).  
    2. File /home/user/helpdesk/helpdesk.db exists and is a valid SQLite database with one table ‘tickets’.  
    3. Table creation SQL:
        CREATE TABLE tickets (
            id      INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            title   TEXT,
            status  TEXT
        );

    4. Rows pre-loaded:
        INSERT INTO tickets VALUES
            (1,'Cannot connect to VPN','open'),
            (2,'Email not syncing','open'),
            (3,'Forgot password','resolved'),
            (4,'Printer jam','in_progress');

    Expected state after correct task completion:

    1. Row with id 4 is updated to:
        4 | Printer jam | resolved

    2. No other rows are changed or added.

    3. /home/user/helpdesk/ticket_summary.log exists (permissions 644) and its exact, newline-terminated content is:

        Total Tickets: 4
        Open Tickets: 2
        In-Progress Tickets: 0
        Resolved Tickets: 2

        (There is a single trailing newline after the last line and no carriage returns).

    The grader will:
    • Query helpdesk.db to ensure ticket id 4 status == 'resolved'.  
    • Read ticket_summary.log and compare byte-for-byte with the block above.  
    • Optionally cross-check that the counts match a fresh SQL COUNT(*) aggregation.
